title: "buildah add" and "buildah copy" will attempt to access excluded paths
body:
### Issue Description

Passing `--exclude` to `buildah add` or `buildah copy` will not keep buildah from trying to accessing the excluded paths, causing it to fail it there are not enough permissions.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Mount an ext4 filesystem in `<mount-path>` (it should contain a `lost+found` directory only readable by root)
2. `buildah from --name test scratch`
3. `buildah add --exclude "**/lost+found" test <mount-path> /` (or `buildah copy`)



### Describe the results you received

```
Error: adding content to container "test": reading "<mount-path>": copier: get: "<mount-path>"("<mount-path>"): opening directory for adding its contents to archive: open <mount-path>/lost+found: permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

Since `--exclude "**/lost+found"` should exclude any file or directory named `lost+found`, it should not be attempting to access `<mount-path>/lost+found`.

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.39.3
Go Version:      go1.24.1
Image Spec:      1.1.0
Runtime Spec:    1.2.0
CNI Spec:        1.1.0

```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
